 2200: def _merge_bibliographies(
 2201:     section_slices: list[list[dict[str, Any]]],
 2202: ) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
 2203:     """Merge per-section biblios into a single ordered bibliography,
 2204:     remapping section-local citation numbers to global numbers."""
 2205:     # Each section's biblio has its own 1-based numbering. We need to
 2206:     # renumber globally, but the section's verified_text already has
 2207:     # [1][2][3] markers in section-local space.
 2208:     # Simpler approach: return the raw per-section biblios flattened,
 2209:     # deduped by evidence_id, and let the caller remap the inline
 2210:     # markers in a separate pass.
 2211:     seen: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
 2212:     for sl in section_slices:
 2213:         for entry in sl:
 2214:             ev_id = entry.get("evidence_id", "")
 2215:             if ev_id and ev_id not in seen:
 2216:                 seen[ev_id] = dict(entry)
 2217:     # Renumber globally
 2218:     final: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
 2219:     for i, entry in enumerate(seen.values(), 1):
 2220:         new_entry = dict(entry)
 2221:         new_entry["num"] = i
 2222:         final.append(new_entry)
 2223:     return final
 2224: 
 2225: 
 2226: def _remap_section_markers_to_global(
 2227:     section_results: list[SectionResult],
 2228:     global_biblio: list[dict[str, Any]],
 2229: ) -> list[str]:
 2230:     """Rewrite each section's [N] markers from section-local to global.
 2231: 
 2232:     Returns a list of remapped section prose strings.
 2233:     """
 2234:     ev_to_global = {b["evidence_id"]: b["num"] for b in global_biblio}
 2235:     remapped: list[str] = []
 2236:     for sect in section_results:
 2237:         if not sect.verified_text:
 2238:             continue
 2239:         # Build a mapping section-local-num -> global-num
 2240:         local_to_global: dict[int, int] = {}
 2241:         for entry in sect.biblio_slice:
 2242:             local_num = entry.get("num")
 2243:             ev_id = entry.get("evidence_id", "")
 2244:             global_num = ev_to_global.get(ev_id)
 2245:             if local_num is not None and global_num is not None:
 2246:                 local_to_global[local_num] = global_num
 2247:         text = sect.verified_text
 2248: 
 2249:         # Replace [N] markers using the mapping. Do the replace with a
 2250:         # callable to avoid subsequent substitutions clobbering each
 2251:         # other (e.g., [1] -> [5] -> [15]).
 2252:         def _replace(match: re.Match) -> str:
 2253:             n = int(match.group(1))
 2254:             g = local_to_global.get(n)
 2255:             return f"[{g}]" if g else match.group(0)

 1129:

 1132:     the prompt for sections whose subject/predicate keywords match

 1149:     ]

 1169:         cross_trial_block=cross_trial_block,
 1170:     )
 1171:     total_in_tok += in_tok
 1172:     total_out_tok += out_tok

src/polaris_graph\generator\multi_section_generator.py:237:- **M-40: Mechanism section is the narrative-depth lever.** When AT LEAST 3 evidence rows in the summary above contain mechanism-of-action vocabulary — in either the `title:` field or the statement body — you MUST include "Mechanism" as one of the outline sections (5 by default, 6 when regulatory evidence is also present per M-41a above). Trigger vocabulary (any of, case-insensitive): "mechanism", "pharmacokinetic", "pharmacodynamic", "receptor", "half-life", "bioavailability", "metabolism", "agonist", "antagonist", "binding", "signaling", "pathway", "kinetic". A research-grade synthesis explains WHY the intervention works, not only WHETHER it works. Top-tier Deep Research outputs (GPT-5.4 DR, Gemini 3.1 Pro DR) dedicate a full section to mechanism/pharmacology for any clinical efficacy question; a report without it reads as a short brief rather than a deep synthesis. This rule is generalizable: in materials/chemistry a Mechanism section covers reaction pathway / phase transition / interface chemistry; in policy it covers causal pathway / incentive mechanism / enforcement mechanism; in finance it covers transmission channel / market microstructure.

  304: 
  305: def wrap_evidence_for_prompt(
  306:     evidence_id: str,
  307:     statement: str,
  308:     direct_quote: str,
  309:     source_url: str = "",
  310:     tier: str = "",
  311: ) -> str:
  312:     """Wrap evidence in the delimited form the generator expects.
  313: 
  314:     Uses fixed delimiters `<<<evidence:...>>>` and `<<<end_evidence>>>`.
  315:     These are deliberately not standard XML so that a bad actor who
  316:     tries to spoof closing tags can't trivially break out.
  317:     """
  318:     statement_s, r1 = sanitize_evidence_text(statement)
  319:     quote_s, r2 = sanitize_evidence_text(direct_quote)
  320:     total_red = r1 + r2
  321:     if total_red > 0:
  322:         logger.warning(
  323:             "[provenance_generator] Redacted %d prompt-injection pattern(s) "
  324:             "from evidence %s",
  325:             total_red, evidence_id,
  326:         )
  327:     return (
  328:         f"<<<evidence:{evidence_id}>>>\n"
  329:         f"tier: {tier}\n"
  330:         f"url: {source_url}\n"
  331:         f"statement: {statement_s}\n"
  332:         f"direct_quote: {quote_s}\n"
  333:         f"<<<end_evidence>>>"
  334:     )
  335: 
  336: 
  337: # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  338: # Provenance token parsing
  339: # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  340: 
  341: # Pattern: [#ev:<evidence_id>:<start>-<end>]
  342: # Example: [#ev:ev_step1:12-45]
  343: _PROVENANCE_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(
  344:     r"\[#ev:(?P<ev_id>[A-Za-z0-9_]+):(?P<start>\d+)-(?P<end>\d+)\]"
  345: )
  346: 
  347: 
  348: @dataclass
  349: class ProvenanceToken:
  350:     evidence_id: str
  351:     start: int
  352:     end: int
  353:     raw: str
  354: 
  355:     @property
  356:     def span_len(self) -> int:
  357:         return self.end - self.start
  358: 
  359: 
  360: @dataclass
  361: class SentenceVerification:
  362:     sentence: str
  363:     tokens: list[ProvenanceToken]
  364:     is_verified: bool
  365:     failure_reasons: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
  366:     resolved_citation_marker: str = ""   # e.g., "[1]"
  367:     # Gap-2: soft warnings that don't trigger a drop but get surfaced
  368:     # to the evaluator (stored as PT13 in external_evaluator).
  369:     soft_warnings: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
  370:

 1025: 
 1026: def resolve_provenance_to_citations(
 1027:     kept_sentences: list[SentenceVerification],
 1028:     evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
 1029: ) -> tuple[str, list[dict[str, Any]]]:
 1030:     """Strip [#ev:...] tokens and replace with numbered citations.
 1031: 
 1032:     Returns (rendered_text, bibliography_list). Bibliography is a list
 1033:     of dicts: {num, evidence_id, url, tier, statement}.
 1034:     """
 1035:     ev_to_num: dict[str, int] = {}
 1036:     biblio: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
 1037: 
 1038:     def _num_for(ev_id: str) -> int:
 1039:         if ev_id not in ev_to_num:
 1040:             ev_to_num[ev_id] = len(ev_to_num) + 1
 1041:             ev = evidence_pool.get(ev_id, {})
 1042:             biblio.append({
 1043:                 "num": ev_to_num[ev_id],
 1044:                 "evidence_id": ev_id,
 1045:                 "url": ev.get("source_url", ""),
 1046:                 "tier": ev.get("tier", ""),
 1047:                 "statement": (ev.get("statement") or "")[:300],
 1048:             })
 1049:         return ev_to_num[ev_id]
 1050: 
 1051:     findings_lines: list[str] = []
 1052:     limitations_lines: list[str] = []
 1053:     for sv in kept_sentences:
 1054:         # Strip provenance tokens first so degenerate fragments can be
 1055:         # detected before we assign citation numbers (otherwise the
 1056:         # bibliography keeps an entry whose only citing sentence we
 1057:         # later drop).
 1058:         stripped = _PROVENANCE_TOKEN_RE.sub("", sv.sentence).strip()
 1059:         # Clean trailing spaces before punctuation
 1060:         stripped = re.sub(r"\s+([.!?,;])", r"\1", stripped)
 1061:         # BUG-M-8 (Codex pass 9): drop degenerate sentence fragments
 1062:         # that survive strict_verify as bare punctuation + citation
 1063:         # (observed in the Novo sweep as ".[4]", "Morgan analysts.[12]",
 1064:         # ".[14]" between legitimate sentences). A real sentence has
 1065:         # ≥3 content words AND ≥15 chars of prose after provenance
 1066:         # stripping. Lower bounds deliberately conservative — the
 1067:         # shortest legitimate research sentences in smoke runs
 1068:         # ("No contradictions detected.") comfortably clear it.
 1069:         _content_w = re.findall(r"[A-Za-z]+", stripped)
 1070:         if len(_content_w) < 3 or len(stripped) < 15:
 1071:             continue
 1072:         # Assign citation numbers only for surviving sentences
 1073:         used_nums: list[int] = []
 1074:         for tok in sv.tokens:
 1075:             n = _num_for(tok.evidence_id)
 1076:             if n not in used_nums:
 1077:                 used_nums.append(n)
 1078:         # Append citation markers
 1079:         markers = "".join(f"[{n}]" for n in used_nums)
 1080:         sentence_out = stripped + markers
 1081: 
 1082:         # Gap-3: put Limitations sentences in a separate paragraph so
 1083:         # they render on their own line in the final report.
 1084:         if any("limitations_paragraph_pass_through" in w for w in sv.soft_warnings):
 1085:             limitations_lines.append(sentence_out)

  609:     """Verify every provenance token in a sentence.
  610: 
  611:     Checks:
  612:       1. Evidence ID exists in pool.
  613:       2. Span bounds are within evidence direct_quote length.
  614:       3. If require_number_match AND the sentence contains numbers,

  618:     failures: list[str] = []

  620:     # Strip provenance tokens for numeric matching to avoid matching

  622:     sentence_for_numbers = _PROVENANCE_TOKEN_RE.sub(" ", sentence).strip()

  624:     if not tokens:
  625:         # Sentences without provenance are rejected outright.

  627:             sentence=sentence,

  633:     # Fix-3b: When a sentence has multiple citations (e.g.,
  634:     # [#ev:a][#ev:b][#ev:c]), the decimals in the sentence may come
  635:     # from DIFFERENT cited spans — the ICER trial's 86.6% treatment

  637:     # verifier should require that each decimal in the sentence is
  638:     # found in SOME cited span (union), not in every span.
  639:     #
  640:     # Also: placebo-arm numbers (e.g., "vs 2.4% with placebo") and
  641:     # achievement thresholds ("≥5%") are filtered from the set of
  642:     # decimals we require to verify — they're comparator/structural,
  643:     # not the claim itself.

  645:     aggregated_span_text: list[str] = []

  653:         if tok.end > len(direct_quote):

   44: def _load_uploaded_documents(
   45:     document_ids: list[str], ingester: Any = None, chunk_size: int = 1500,
   46: ) -> list[dict]:
   47:     """Load chunks for given document_ids from DocumentIngester.
   48: 
   49:     Per I-f3-001. Validates each doc_id against 16-hex format (matches
   50:     `hashlib.sha256(file_bytes).hexdigest()[:16]` per document_ingester.py:162)
   51:     BEFORE filesystem lookup to prevent path traversal. Skips invalid /
   52:     missing IDs with a logged warning. Raises RuntimeError if every
   53:     requested ID failed (LAW II — fail loud).
   54:     """
   55:     if not document_ids:
   56:         return []
   57:     if ingester is None:
   58:         from src.polaris_graph.document_ingester import DocumentIngester
   59:         ingester = DocumentIngester()
   60:     out: list[dict] = []
   61:     for doc_id in document_ids:
   62:         if not _DOC_ID_RE.fullmatch(doc_id):
   63:             logger.warning("[v4 graph] invalid doc_id format: %r", doc_id)
   64:             continue
   65:         doc = ingester.get_document(doc_id)
   66:         if doc is None:
   67:             logger.warning("[v4 graph] doc_id %s not found", doc_id)
   68:             continue
   69:         content = doc.get("content", "")
   70:         if not content:
   71:             logger.warning("[v4 graph] doc_id %s has empty content", doc_id)
   72:             continue
   73:         meta = doc.get("metadata", {})
   74:         name = meta.get("original_filename") or meta.get("filename") or doc_id
   75:         chunks = [content[i : i + chunk_size] for i in range(0, len(content), chunk_size)]
   76:         for idx, chunk_text in enumerate(chunks):
   77:             out.append({
   78:                 "document_id": doc_id, "filename": name,
   79:                 "chunk_index": idx, "text": chunk_text,
   80:             })
   81:     if not out:
   82:         raise RuntimeError(
   83:             f"_load_uploaded_documents: every requested document_id "
   84:             f"({len(document_ids)} ids) failed to resolve"
   85:         )
   86:     return out

  112: def _infer_domain(application: str, query: str) -> str:
  113:     """Return a scope-template domain. Default to 'custom' for
  114:     free-form UI queries that don't hint at clinical/tech/policy/dd."""
  115:     a = (application or "").lower()
  116:     if a in _DOMAIN_HINTS:

  118:     q = (query or "").lower()
  119:     for key, dom in _DOMAIN_HINTS.items():
  120:         if key in q:
  121:             return dom
  122:     return "custom"

  - "Resolve/validate document_ids before insert_run/enqueue so a missing or unusable upload does not leave a queued orphan run."
  - "Queued/unparsed pdf/docx uploads with no content should fail loud for #506, not silently produce zero upload evidence."

   58: def _derive_slug(template_id: str, question: str) -> str:
   59:     """Deterministic URL-safe slug for pipeline-A run_dir nesting.
   60: 
   61:     Pipeline-A reads q['slug']; this produces a stable, human-readable
   62:     identifier per (template, question) pair. UUID provides uniqueness
   63:     via the artifact_dir parent — slug itself doesn't need to be unique.
   64:     """
   65:     base = f"{template_id}_{question[:60]}"
   66:     cleaned = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9_]+", "_", base.lower()).strip("_")
   67:     return cleaned[:120] or "untitled"
   68: 
   69: 
   70: @dramatiq.actor(max_retries=ENQUEUE_MAX_RETRIES, time_limit=30 * 60 * 1000)
   71: def enqueue_research_run(run_id: str, request_payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
   72:     """Execute a research run via pipeline-A. Idempotent on run_id.
   73: 
   74:     Stub-mode path: when run_store has no row for run_id (tests use
   75:     .fn() directly without insert_run), returns deterministic noop
   76:     without DB writes — preserves I-phase0-005 stub-mode semantics.
   77: 
   78:     Production path: marks in_progress, builds q-dict with v6 fields,
   79:     invokes scripts.run_honest_sweep_r3.run_one_query, parses the
   80:     manifest.json pipeline-A writes, and dispatches to

 succeeded in 763ms:
    1: """POST /upload — F3b drag-drop document upload endpoint.
    2: 
    3: Per docs/carney_delivery_plan_v6_2.md F3b, the user can upload PDFs /
    4: docx / md / txt to seed a research run. Phase 0 ships the contract:
    5: returns a document_id + parse_status; Phase 1 wires actual parsing +
    6: chunking + sovereignty router (CAN_REAL data must stay on Canadian
    7: infra).
    8: 
    9: Per CLAUDE.md security posture: file content classification
   10: (PUBLIC_SYNTHETIC | CAN_REAL | PRIVATE | CLIENT | UNKNOWN) is set by
   11: the caller; default is UNKNOWN and triggers conservative routing.
   12: 
   13: I-rdy-010 (#506): `get_upload_record` + `chunk_text` let POST /runs
   14: resolve uploaded document_ids to content at run-creation time. The
   15: upload table is an in-process dict — the Dramatiq worker is a separate
   16: process and cannot read it — so /runs (which runs in the API process)
   17: resolves the content and embeds it in the actor message.
   18: """
   19: 
   20: from __future__ import annotations
   21: 
   22: import hashlib
   23: import html as _html
   24: import uuid
   25: from typing import Literal
   26: 
   27: from fastapi import APIRouter, Form, HTTPException, UploadFile
   28: from pydantic import BaseModel
   29: 
   30: router = APIRouter(prefix="/upload", tags=["upload"])
   31: 
   32: DataClassification = Literal[
   33:     "PUBLIC_SYNTHETIC",
   34:     "CAN_REAL",
   35:     "PRIVATE",
   36:     "CLIENT",
   37:     "UNKNOWN",
   38: ]
   39: 
   40: ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS = {".pdf", ".docx", ".md", ".txt"}
   41: MAX_BYTES = 50 * 1024 * 1024  # 50 MB per upload (I-f3-005 frontend dropzone)
   42: 
   43: # I-rdy-010 (#506): grounding-chunk parameters. CHUNK_SIZE matches the
   44: # upload-endpoint preview chunking; MAX_GROUNDING_CHUNKS bounds how much
   45: # uploaded text is embedded into a Dramatiq actor message — a .md/.txt
   46: # upload can be up to MAX_BYTES, so the full document is NOT embedded.
   47: CHUNK_SIZE = 280
   48: MAX_GROUNDING_CHUNKS = 40
   49: 
   50: 
   51: class UploadResponse(BaseModel):
   52:     document_id: str
   53:     filename: str
   54:     bytes: int
   55:     sha256: str
   56:     classification: DataClassification
   57:     parse_status: Literal["queued", "completed", "failed"]
   58:     chunk_preview: list[str]
   59:     content: str = ""
   60:     html: str = ""
   61: 
   62: 
   63: _UPLOAD_TABLE: dict[str, UploadResponse] = {}
   64: 
   65: 
   66: def chunk_text(
   67:     text: str,
   68:     *,
   69:     size: int = CHUNK_SIZE,
   70:     max_chunks: int = MAX_GROUNDING_CHUNKS,
   71: ) -> list[str]:
   72:     """Split text into fixed-size chunks, capped at ``max_chunks``.
   73: 
   74:     The cap bounds the embedded actor-message payload (I-rdy-010) — an
   75:     uploaded .md/.txt document can be up to ``MAX_BYTES``, so only the
   76:     leading ``max_chunks`` are used to ground a run.
   77:     """
   78:     text = text.strip()
   79:     if not text:
   80:         return []
   81:     chunks = [text[i : i + size] for i in range(0, len(text), size)]
   82:     return chunks[:max_chunks]
   83: 
   84: 
   85: def get_upload_record(document_id: str) -> UploadResponse | None:
   86:     """Look up an uploaded document by id, or None if absent.
   87: 
   88:     Used by POST /runs (I-rdy-010) to resolve document_ids → content at
   89:     run-creation time, in the API process that owns ``_UPLOAD_TABLE``.
   90:     """
   91:     return _UPLOAD_TABLE.get(document_id)
   92: 
   93: 
   94: @router.post("", response_model=UploadResponse, status_code=201)
   95: async def upload_document(
   96:     file: UploadFile,
   97:     classification: DataClassification = Form("UNKNOWN"),
   98: ) -> UploadResponse:
   99:     if file.filename is None:
  100:         raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="filename required")
  101: 
  102:     ext = "." + file.filename.rsplit(".", 1)[-1].lower() if "." in file.filename else ""
  103:     if ext not in ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS:
  104:         raise HTTPException(
  105:             status_code=415,
  106:             detail=f"Unsupported extension {ext!r}. Allowed: {sorted(ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS)}",
  107:         )
  108: 
  109:     content = await file.read()
  110:     if len(content) > MAX_BYTES:
  111:         raise HTTPException(
  112:             status_code=413,
  113:             detail=f"File exceeds {MAX_BYTES // (1024 * 1024)} MB limit",
  114:         )
  115:     if len(content) == 0:
  116:         raise HTTPException(status_code=422, detail="empty file")
  117: 
  118:     sha = hashlib.sha256(content).hexdigest()
  119:     document_id = uuid.uuid4().hex
  120: 
  121:     if ext in {".md", ".txt"}:
  122:         try:
  123:             text = content.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")
  124:         except Exception:
  125:             text = ""
  126:         chunks = [text[i : i + 280] for i in range(0, min(len(text), 840), 280) if text]
  127:         preview_text = text
  128:         preview_html = f"<pre>{_html.escape(text)}</pre>" if text else ""
  129:     else:
  130:         chunks = []
  131:         preview_text = ""
  132:         preview_html = ""
  133: 
  134:     response = UploadResponse(
  135:         document_id=document_id,
  136:         filename=file.filename,
  137:         bytes=len(content),
  138:         sha256=sha,
  139:         classification=classification,
  140:         parse_status="completed" if chunks else "queued",
  141:         chunk_preview=chunks[:3],
  142:         content=preview_text,
  143:         html=preview_html,
  144:     )
  145:     _UPLOAD_TABLE[document_id] = response
  146:     return response
  147: 
  148: 
  149: @router.get("/{document_id}", response_model=UploadResponse)
  150: def get_upload(document_id: str) -> UploadResponse:
  151:     record = _UPLOAD_TABLE.get(document_id)
  152:     if record is None:
  153:         raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f"document {document_id!r} not found")
  154:     return record

 succeeded in 761ms:
    1: """Runs router — POST /runs, GET /runs/{id}.
    2: 
    3: I-phase0-005 (Codex APPROVE iter 4): persist runs to SQLite via
    4: polaris_v6.queue.run_store and enqueue the Dramatiq actor on POST.
    5: Replaces the previous in-memory `_run_table` dict.
    6: 
    7: I-rdy-010 (#506): POST /runs resolves uploaded `document_ids` to content
    8: BEFORE inserting/enqueuing the run, so a missing or unparsed upload fails
    9: loud here rather than leaving a queued orphan run. The resolved content is
   10: embedded in the actor message because the Dramatiq worker is a separate
   11: process and cannot read the API process's in-memory upload table.
   12: """
   13: 
   14: from __future__ import annotations
   15: 
   16: import sqlite3
   17: import uuid
   18: 
   19: from fastapi import APIRouter, HTTPException
   20: 
   21: from polaris_v6.api.upload import chunk_text, get_upload_record
   22: from polaris_v6.queue import run_store
   23: from polaris_v6.queue.actors import enqueue_research_run
   24: from polaris_v6.schemas.run_request import RunRequest
   25: from polaris_v6.schemas.run_status import RunStatusResponse
   26: 
   27: router = APIRouter(prefix="/runs", tags=["runs"])
   28: 
   29: 
   30: def _resolve_uploaded_documents(document_ids: list[str]) -> list[dict]:
   31:     """Resolve uploaded document_ids → content for actor-message embedding.
   32: 
   33:     Returns one dict per id: `{document_id, classification, filename,
   34:     chunks}`. `chunks` is `chunk_text(record.content)` — the full document
   35:     re-chunked (and capped), NOT the 3-chunk `chunk_preview`.
   36: 
   37:     Fails loud (HTTP 400) per LAW II on:
   38:       * a document_id with no upload record;
   39:       * an upload that yields no extractable text (e.g. an unparsed
   40:         pdf/docx) — a silent zero-evidence run would mislead the operator.
   41:     """
   42:     resolved: list[dict] = []
   43:     for document_id in document_ids:
   44:         record = get_upload_record(document_id)
   45:         if record is None:
   46:             raise HTTPException(
   47:                 status_code=400,
   48:                 detail=f"uploaded document {document_id!r} not found",
   49:             )
   50:         chunks = chunk_text(record.content)
   51:         if not chunks:
   52:             raise HTTPException(
   53:                 status_code=400,
   54:                 detail=(
   55:                     f"uploaded document {document_id!r} ({record.filename}) "
   56:                     "has no extractable text — pdf/docx parsing is not yet "
   57:                     "available; upload a .md or .txt document to ground a run."
   58:                 ),
   59:             )
   60:         resolved.append(
   61:             {
   62:                 "document_id": document_id,
   63:                 "classification": record.classification,
   64:                 "filename": record.filename,
   65:                 "chunks": chunks,
   66:             }
   67:         )
   68:     return resolved
   69: 
   70: 
   71: @router.post("", response_model=RunStatusResponse, status_code=202)
   72: def create_run(payload: RunRequest) -> RunStatusResponse:
   73:     # I-rdy-010: resolve uploads BEFORE insert_run/enqueue so a bad
   74:     # document_id fails loud here instead of orphaning a queued run.
   75:     uploaded_documents = _resolve_uploaded_documents(payload.document_ids)
   76: 
   77:     run_id = uuid.uuid4().hex
   78:     try:
   79:         run_store.insert_run(run_id, payload.template, payload.question)
   80:     except sqlite3.IntegrityError as exc:
   81:         # uuid4 collision is theoretically impossible but defensive
   82:         raise HTTPException(status_code=409, detail=f"run {run_id!r} already exists") from exc
   83:     except RuntimeError as exc:
   84:         # init_db permission-denied or similar
   85:         raise HTTPException(status_code=503, detail=str(exc)) from exc
   86: 
   87:     # The actor message carries the resolved upload content (the worker is
   88:     # a separate process — it cannot read the API process's upload table).
   89:     actor_payload = payload.model_dump()
   90:     actor_payload["uploaded_documents"] = uploaded_documents
   91:     enqueue_research_run.send(run_id, actor_payload)
   92: 
   93:     record = run_store.get_run(run_id)
   94:     if record is None:  # pragma: no cover — insert just succeeded
   95:         raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail="run row vanished after insert")
   96:     return record
   97: 
   98: 
   99: @router.get("/{run_id}", response_model=RunStatusResponse)
  100: def get_run(run_id: str) -> RunStatusResponse:
  101:     record = run_store.get_run(run_id)
  102:     if record is None:
  103:         raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f"run {run_id!r} not found")
  104:     return record

codex

   35: import os
   36: import re
   37: import time
   38: from pathlib import Path
   39: from typing import Any, Callable, Optional
   40: 
   41: _DOC_ID_RE = re.compile(r"[a-f0-9]{16}")
   42: 
   43: 
   44: def _load_uploaded_documents(
   45:     document_ids: list[str], ingester: Any = None, chunk_size: int = 1500,
   46: ) -> list[dict]:
   47:     """Load chunks for given document_ids from DocumentIngester.
   48: 
   49:     Per I-f3-001. Validates each doc_id against 16-hex format (matches
   50:     `hashlib.sha256(file_bytes).hexdigest()[:16]` per document_ingester.py:162)
   51:     BEFORE filesystem lookup to prevent path traversal. Skips invalid /
   52:     missing IDs with a logged warning. Raises RuntimeError if every
   53:     requested ID failed (LAW II — fail loud).
   54:     """
   55:     if not document_ids:
   56:         return []
   57:     if ingester is None:
   58:         from src.polaris_graph.document_ingester import DocumentIngester
   59:         ingester = DocumentIngester()
   60:     out: list[dict] = []
   61:     for doc_id in document_ids:
   62:         if not _DOC_ID_RE.fullmatch(doc_id):
   63:             logger.warning("[v4 graph] invalid doc_id format: %r", doc_id)
   64:             continue
   65:         doc = ingester.get_document(doc_id)
   66:         if doc is None:
   67:             logger.warning("[v4 graph] doc_id %s not found", doc_id)
   68:             continue
   69:         content = doc.get("content", "")
   70:         if not content:
   71:             logger.warning("[v4 graph] doc_id %s has empty content", doc_id)
   72:             continue
   73:         meta = doc.get("metadata", {})
   74:         name = meta.get("original_filename") or meta.get("filename") or doc_id
   75:         chunks = [content[i : i + chunk_size] for i in range(0, len(content), chunk_size)]
   76:         for idx, chunk_text in enumerate(chunks):
   77:             out.append({
   78:                 "document_id": doc_id, "filename": name,
   79:                 "chunk_index": idx, "text": chunk_text,
   80:             })
   81:     if not out:
   82:         raise RuntimeError(
   83:             f"_load_uploaded_documents: every requested document_id "
   84:             f"({len(document_ids)} ids) failed to resolve"
   85:         )
   86:     return out
   87: 
   88:

  112: def _infer_domain(application: str, query: str) -> str:
  113:     """Return a scope-template domain. Default to 'custom' for
  114:     free-form UI queries that don't hint at clinical/tech/policy/dd."""
  115:     a = (application or "").lower()
  116:     if a in _DOMAIN_HINTS:
  117:         return _DOMAIN_HINTS[a]
  118:     q = (query or "").lower()
  119:     for key, dom in _DOMAIN_HINTS.items():
  120:         if key in q:
  121:             return dom
  122:     return "custom"

  125: def _safe_load_json(path: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:
  126:     """Best-effort JSON load; empty dict if missing / malformed."""
  127:     if not path.exists():
  128:         return {}
  129:     try:
  130:         return json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
  131:     except Exception:
  132:         return {}

  135: def _adapt_pipeline_a_to_ui_json(

  139: ) -> dict[str, Any]:

  152:     contradictions = _safe_load_json(run_dir / "contradictions.json")

  155:     status = manifest.get("status", summary.get("status", "unknown"))

  182:             "completed": datetime.datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc).isoformat(),

 succeeded in 859ms:
    1: """Sovereignty router: enforces EXTERNAL_LEAK_FORBIDDEN policy at call sites (I-f3-003).
    2: 
    3: Per Carney v6.2 §332. The router is a policy library — callers invoke
    4: `assert_safe_for_external` (strict gate) or `filter_for_external_egress`
    5: (split mode) before any outbound payload leaves Canadian-sovereign infra.
    6: """
    7: 
    8: from __future__ import annotations
    9: 
   10: from dataclasses import dataclass
   11: from typing import Any, Iterable
   12: 
   13: from polaris_graph.sovereignty.classification import (
   14:     is_external_leak_forbidden,
   15:     parse_classification,
   16: )
   17: 
   18: 
   19: @dataclass(frozen=True)
   20: class SovereigntyDecision:
   21:     """Result of `filter_for_external_egress(strict=False)`.
   22: 
   23:     Note: while the dataclass is frozen, the contained `tuple` fields
   24:     are themselves immutable.
   25:     """
   26: 
   27:     allowed: tuple
   28:     blocked: tuple
   29:     reasons: tuple[str, ...]
   30: 
   31: 
   32: class SovereigntyViolationError(RuntimeError):
   33:     """Raised in strict mode when any item is forbidden external-egress."""
   34: 
   35: 
   36: def _classification_of(item: Any) -> str | None:
   37:     raw = getattr(item, "classification", None)
   38:     if raw is None and isinstance(item, dict):
   39:         raw = item.get("classification")
   40:     return raw
   41: 
   42: 
   43: def filter_for_external_egress(
   44:     items: Iterable, *, strict: bool = True,
   45: ) -> SovereigntyDecision:
   46:     """Filter items against EXTERNAL_LEAK_FORBIDDEN per Carney v6.2 §332.
   47: 
   48:     strict=True (default): raises SovereigntyViolationError on first forbidden item.
   49:     strict=False: returns SovereigntyDecision split.
   50: 
   51:     Items lacking `classification` default to UNKNOWN (forbidden).
   52:     """
   53:     allowed: list = []
   54:     blocked: list = []
   55:     reasons: list[str] = []
   56:     for item in items:
   57:         cls = parse_classification(_classification_of(item))
   58:         if is_external_leak_forbidden(cls):
   59:             reason = f"classification={cls.value} forbidden external-egress"
   60:             if strict:
   61:                 raise SovereigntyViolationError(reason)
   62:             blocked.append(item)
   63:             reasons.append(reason)
   64:         else:
   65:             allowed.append(item)
   66:     return SovereigntyDecision(
   67:         allowed=tuple(allowed), blocked=tuple(blocked), reasons=tuple(reasons),
   68:     )
   69: 
   70: 
   71: def assert_safe_for_external(items: Iterable) -> None:
   72:     """Strict gate; raises SovereigntyViolationError on any forbidden item."""
   73:     filter_for_external_egress(items, strict=True)

 succeeded in 891ms:
    1: """Canonical data classification taxonomy + sovereignty policy (I-f3-002).
    2: 
    3: Codifies `docs/carney_delivery_plan_v6_2.md:332` "All non-PUBLIC_SYNTHETIC
    4: classifications blocked from external API". The `EXTERNAL_LEAK_FORBIDDEN`
    5: set is the authoritative external-egress policy consumed by the
    6: sovereignty router (I-f3-003).
    7: """
    8: 
    9: from __future__ import annotations
   10: 
   11: from enum import Enum
   12: from typing import Union
   13: 
   14: 
   15: class DataClassification(str, Enum):
   16:     PUBLIC_SYNTHETIC = "PUBLIC_SYNTHETIC"
   17:     CAN_REAL = "CAN_REAL"
   18:     PRIVATE = "PRIVATE"
   19:     CLIENT = "CLIENT"
   20:     UNKNOWN = "UNKNOWN"
   21: 
   22: 
   23: ALL_CLASSIFICATIONS: tuple[DataClassification, ...] = tuple(DataClassification)
   24: 
   25: EXTERNAL_LEAK_FORBIDDEN: frozenset[DataClassification] = frozenset(
   26:     {
   27:         DataClassification.CAN_REAL,
   28:         DataClassification.PRIVATE,
   29:         DataClassification.CLIENT,
   30:         DataClassification.UNKNOWN,
   31:     }
   32: )
   33: 
   34: 
   35: def parse_classification(
   36:     value: Union[str, DataClassification, None],
   37: ) -> DataClassification:
   38:     """Normalize input to DataClassification. None → UNKNOWN. Invalid → ValueError."""
   39:     if value is None:
   40:         return DataClassification.UNKNOWN
   41:     if isinstance(value, DataClassification):
   42:         return value
   43:     return DataClassification(value)
   44: 
   45: 
   46: def is_external_leak_forbidden(classification: DataClassification) -> bool:
   47:     """True iff the given classification is forbidden from external-API egress."""
   48:     return classification in EXTERNAL_LEAK_FORBIDDEN

  403: def _build_deterministic_fallback_outline(
  404:     evidence: list[dict[str, Any]],
  405: ) -> list[SectionPlan]:
  406:     """BUG-M-203 fix (deep-dive R4): deterministic 3-section fallback
  407:     when the planner collapses. Uses round-robin evidence assignment
  408:     to three allowed titles so each section has >=2 unique,
  409:     non-overlapping evidence IDs. Returns [] if evidence is insufficient.
  410:     """
  411:     ev_ids = [ev.get("evidence_id", "") for ev in evidence]
  412:     ev_ids = [e for e in ev_ids if e]  # drop empty
  413:     # Need at least 6 unique IDs to guarantee 3 sections with >=2 each.
  414:     if len(set(ev_ids)) < 6:
  415:         return []
  416: 
  417:     titles = ["Efficacy", "Safety", "Comparative"]
  418:     # Filter to titles that exist in the allowed list.
  419:     allowed_titles = [t for t in titles if t in _ALLOWED_SECTIONS]
  420:     if len(allowed_titles) < 3:
  421:         # Extremely defensive; the three titles above are in the canonical set.
  422:         return []
  423: 
  424:     focuses = {
  425:         "Efficacy": "Summarize the efficacy endpoints supported by the evidence.",
  426:         "Safety": "Summarize the safety signals and adverse-event profile.",
  427:         "Comparative": (
  428:             "Summarize comparisons against alternative interventions "
  429:             "when evidence supports such comparison."
  430:         ),
  431:     }
  432: 
  433:     # Round-robin: section i gets ev_ids[i::3], capped at 30 per section.
  434:     # M-24 fix: Without the cap, a 289-row corpus produces 96 ev_ids per
  435:     # section; inlining 96 evidence blocks in the section prompt created
  436:     # >100K-token request bodies that OpenRouter rejects as 400 Bad Request
  437:     # (V10 FATAL 2026-04-19). Cap at 30 keeps per-section prompts within
  438:     # DeepSeek V3.2-Exp's effective request limit while still giving the
  439:     # section writer a rich citation pool.
  440:     _MAX_EV_PER_FALLBACK_SECTION = 30
  441:     plans: list[SectionPlan] = []
  442:     for i, title in enumerate(allowed_titles):
  443:         section_ev = ev_ids[i::3][:_MAX_EV_PER_FALLBACK_SECTION]
  444:         if len(section_ev) < 2:
  445:             # If slicing leaves a section too thin, bail out.
  446:             return []
  447:         plans.append(SectionPlan(
  448:             title=title,
  449:             focus=focuses[title],
  450:             ev_ids=section_ev,
  451:         ))
  452:     return plans
  453:

  455: async def _call_outline(
  456:     research_question: str,

  463:     """Call the planner. Returns (parse_result, retry_attempted, in_tok, out_tok).
  464: 
  465:     BUG-M-203 fix (deep-dive R4): one retry with a tighter prompt when
  466:     validation fails. Retries are capped at 1.
  467:     """
  468:     from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import OpenRouterClient
  469: 
  470:     # Build a compact evidence summary (title + tier + 160 chars of
  471:     # statement). M-40 pass-2 (Codex audit medium): previously the
  472:     # summary omitted the title field, which meant outline rules that
  473:     # trigger on title vocabulary (M-40 Mechanism rule) couldn't fire
  474:     # when the mechanism term lived only in the source title — the
  475:     # LLM literally didn't see it. Title is now included (truncated to
  476:     # 120 chars) so trigger-vocabulary rules can match against title
  477:     # text. Minor increase in prompt size (~60 extra chars per row).
  478:     summary_blocks = []
  479:     for ev in evidence:
  480:         ev_id = ev.get("evidence_id", "")
  481:         title = (ev.get("title", "") or "")[:120]
  482:         stmt = (ev.get("statement", "") or "")[:160]
  483:         tier = ev.get("tier", "")
  484:         # Sanitize via the provenance sanitizer (both title and stmt).
  485:         title_clean, _ = sanitize_evidence_text(title)
  486:         stmt_clean, _ = sanitize_evidence_text(stmt)
  487:         if title_clean:
  488:             summary_blocks.append(
  489:                 f"{ev_id} [{tier}] | title: {title_clean} | {stmt_clean}"
  490:             )
  491:         else:
  492:             summary_blocks.append(f"{ev_id} [{tier}]: {stmt_clean}")
  493:     summary_text = "\n".join(summary_blocks)
  494: 
  495:     prompt = (
  496:         f"Research question: {research_question}\n\n"
  497:         f"Evidence summaries ({len(evidence)} rows):\n"
  498:         f"{summary_text}\n\n"
  499:         f"Return the JSON section plan."
  500:     )
  501: 
  502:     allowed_ev_ids = {ev.get("evidence_id", "") for ev in evidence}
  503:     allowed_ev_ids.discard("")
  504: 
  505:     client = OpenRouterClient(model=model)
  506:     total_in = 0
  507:     total_out = 0
  508:     retry_attempted = False
  509:     try:
  510:         response = await client.generate(
  511:             prompt=prompt,
  512:             system=OUTLINE_SYSTEM_PROMPT,
  513:             max_tokens=max_tokens,
  514:             temperature=temperature,
  515:         )

 1026: def resolve_provenance_to_citations(
 1027:     kept_sentences: list[SentenceVerification],
 1028:     evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
 1029: ) -> tuple[str, list[dict[str, Any]]]:
 1030:     """Strip [#ev:...] tokens and replace with numbered citations.
 1031: 
 1032:     Returns (rendered_text, bibliography_list). Bibliography is a list
 1033:     of dicts: {num, evidence_id, url, tier, statement}.
 1034:     """
 1035:     ev_to_num: dict[str, int] = {}
 1036:     biblio: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
 1037: 
 1038:     def _num_for(ev_id: str) -> int:
 1039:         if ev_id not in ev_to_num:
 1040:             ev_to_num[ev_id] = len(ev_to_num) + 1
 1041:             ev = evidence_pool.get(ev_id, {})
 1042:             biblio.append({
 1043:                 "num": ev_to_num[ev_id],
 1044:                 "evidence_id": ev_id,
 1045:                 "url": ev.get("source_url", ""),
 1046:                 "tier": ev.get("tier", ""),
 1047:                 "statement": (ev.get("statement") or "")[:300],
 1048:             })
 1049:         return ev_to_num[ev_id]
 1050: 
 1051:     findings_lines: list[str] = []
 1052:     limitations_lines: list[str] = []
 1053:     for sv in kept_sentences:
 1054:         # Strip provenance tokens first so degenerate fragments can be
 1055:         # detected before we assign citation numbers (otherwise the
 1056:         # bibliography keeps an entry whose only citing sentence we
 1057:         # later drop).
 1058:         stripped = _PROVENANCE_TOKEN_RE.sub("", sv.sentence).strip()
 1059:         # Clean trailing spaces before punctuation
 1060:         stripped = re.sub(r"\s+([.!?,;])", r"\1", stripped)
 1061:         # BUG-M-8 (Codex pass 9): drop degenerate sentence fragments
 1062:         # that survive strict_verify as bare punctuation + citation
 1063:         # (observed in the Novo sweep as ".[4]", "Morgan analysts.[12]",
 1064:         # ".[14]" between legitimate sentences). A real sentence has
 1065:         # ≥3 content words AND ≥15 chars of prose after provenance
 1066:         # stripping. Lower bounds deliberately conservative — the
 1067:         # shortest legitimate research sentences in smoke runs
 1068:         # ("No contradictions detected.") comfortably clear it.
 1069:         _content_w = re.findall(r"[A-Za-z]+", stripped)
 1070:         if len(_content_w) < 3 or len(stripped) < 15:
 1071:             continue
 1072:         # Assign citation numbers only for surviving sentences
 1073:         used_nums: list[int] = []
 1074:         for tok in sv.tokens:
 1075:             n = _num_for(tok.evidence_id)
 1076:             if n not in used_nums:
 1077:                 used_nums.append(n)
 1078:         # Append citation markers
 1079:         markers = "".join(f"[{n}]" for n in used_nums)
 1080:         sentence_out = stripped + markers
 1081: 
 1082:         # Gap-3: put Limitations sentences in a separate paragraph so
 1083:         # they render on their own line in the final report.
 1084:         if any("limitations_paragraph_pass_through" in w for w in sv.soft_warnings):
 1085:             limitations_lines.append(sentence_out)
